title: Jotform submission edits update in monday.com
excerpt:
I have a Jotform form that can be edited. Whenever a form is submitted, it creates an item in monday.com. 
Using any system (monday.com, Jotform, Make.com, Power Automate, etc.), how can I set that when a submission is e…

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a Jotform form that can be edited. Whenever a form is submitted, it creates an item in monday.com.
Using any system (monday.com, Jotform, Make.com, Power Automate, etc.), how can I set that when a submission is edited on Jotform, it should edit the column values in monday.com, instead of creating a new item.

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Soroh

There are a few native integration recipes for jotform; one of which being:

But that still is an update and not amending existing column values in specific places. You could potentially layer another solution is using an extract app to grab the update and put that into the line item but starts to get more complex from there.

Checkout ‘Superform’ on the app Marketplace:

image1085×561 54 KB

What does pricing look like?

image703×267 11.8 KB

Pretty good

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks great! The problem with this though is that I have an enterprise account at Jotform, and the integration doesn’t work for the enterprise servers (weird, I know).
I can only go from jotform to Monday, and not the opposite way around.
Any other ideas?

posts.author: rroberts (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: I use Power automate to do this if you have Microsoft 365. You need premium Power Automate license and the Power Automate app on Monday. Its very easy to use thought and lower cost than purchasing an integrator.
